item.title: Jones Day Law Firm Says Hackers Accessed Some Clients’ Data
item.description: Hackers have breached Jones Day and accessed the files of 10 clients, the law firm said on Monday.

item.title: US Truck Rates at Highest Since 2022 Add to Inflation Pressures
item.description: Skyrocketing fuel prices due to the Iran war are fanning the embers of transportation costs, which were already rising due to a shrinking pool of drivers in the US.

item.title: Polish Power Prices Turns Negative for Some as Renewables Boom
item.description: Polish power prices turned negative for some customers on two consecutive days as bright, windy weather sparked a surge in renewable electricity supplies that overwhelmed demand.

item.title: Leftist Mello Gets Planning Job, Ending Brazil Central Bank Talk
item.description: Brazil’s new Planning Minister Bruno Moretti will name leftist economist Guilherme Mello as his deputy, removing him from contention for a seat on the central bank’s board and advancing a shakeup of the federal government’s economic team ahead of elections, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Cotton Climbs to Highest Since 2024 on Tight Supply Outlook
item.description: Cotton futures in New York surged to the highest since December 2024 as traders weigh a preliminary government US planting report against an outlook for tighter global supplies this year.

item.title: Goldman Traders See ‘Fast-Money’ Funds Turning Into Stock Buyers
item.description: Systematic investors are poised to flip back into equity-buying mode after slashing their exposure to multi-year lows during the recent market selloff, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s trading desk.

item.title: Why You Should Care About Private Credit
item.description: The swing producer of US financing.

item.title: AMC Shares Gain on Holiday Box Office Surge Buoyed by ‘Mario’
item.description: AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc. saw a surge in ticket sales and concessions over the long Easter weekend, buoyed by The Super Mario Galaxy Movie.

item.title: Wheat Hits One-Week Low With Rains Seen for Dry Kansas Fields
item.description: Wheat fell as rains forecast for this week in the US Plains should help to alleviate drought conditions that have stressed fields.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Line Up Financing for €1.5 Billion Sushi Deal
item.description: Wall Street banks are lending €750 million ($867 million) to finance the roughly €1.5 billion tie-up of Asian food producer Eat Happy Group and sushi supplier Hana Group SAS’ European operations, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trio of Ghost Ships Left in Venezuela Limbo After Maduro’s Exit
item.description: Three dark fleet tankers that loaded Venezuelan oil before the ousting of leader Nicolás Maduro remain stranded in the country, underscoring how its oil industry is still disentangling itself from years of sanctions evasion.

item.title: Binance Compliance Staff Leave Financial Crime, Monitoring Roles
item.description: When Binance pleaded guilty to US sanctions and anti-money-laundering violations in late 2023, rebuilding its compliance operation was key to the deal. The exchange had earlier that year hired Noah Perlman, a former assistant US attorney, as chief compliance officer, to lead teams handling sanctions enforcement, financial crime monitoring and investigations.

item.title: San Francisco House Prices Hit Record $2.15 Million on AI Boom
item.description: San Francisco’s median house price jumped to a record $2.15 million in March, up 18% from a year earlier as wealth generated by artificial intelligence startups flooded the city, according to brokerage Compass Inc.

item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Reverse Losses on Cold Weather, Oil Gains
item.description: US natural gas futures rose as a short bout of cold weather increased demand for the heating and power-plant fuel, after closing down for the previous four sessions. Rising oil prices, which have influenced the front-month US gas contract since the war in Iran broke out, may also be pushing prices higher.

item.title: Sunway Abandons IJM Takeover Bid After Shareholders Reject Offer
item.description: Malaysian conglomerate Sunway Bhd. is abandoning its takeover bid of IJM Corp Bhd. after failing to secure enough support from the target company’s shareholders.

item.title: US Bonds Steady as Traders Bet War Uncertainty Keeps Fed on Hold
item.description: Bond traders kicked off the week betting that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates on hold for the coming year, with the Treasuries market holding steady ahead of President Donald Trump’s extended deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Growth Slows at US Service Providers as Price Gauge Surges
item.description: The US service economy expanded in March at a slower pace as employment shrank by the most since 2023 and input prices accelerated sharply.

item.title: Barings Private Credit Fund Caps Redemptions After 11% Requested
item.description: A Barings LLC private credit fund capped redemptions after investors asked to pull out 11.3% of the shares in the first quarter.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Private Credit Fund Dodges Exodus With 4.999% Redemptions
item.description: A Goldman Sachs Group Inc. private credit fund said investors sought to pull just under 5% of cash in the first quarter, narrowly escaping a broader exodus that has forced peers to cap withdrawals.

item.title: Blackstone-Backed Data Center Firm QTS Kicks Off Bond Sale
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-backed QTS kicked off the sale of a 10-year investment-grade green bond on Monday as the firm builds a major data center in the state of Georgia.

item.title: Senior Housing-Focused REIT National Healthcare Files for US IPO
item.description: National Healthcare Properties Inc., a real estate investment trust, filed for an initial public offering, positioning itself to capitalize on favorable demographic trends associated with a growing elderly population in the US.

item.title: Strategy Posts $14.5 Billion Unrealized Loss in First Quarter
item.description: Bitcoin accumulator Strategy Inc. registered a roughly $14.5 billion unrealized loss in the first quarter as the value of the Michael Saylor-led company’s cryptocurrency holdings fell.

item.title: Israel, Greece Sign €650 Million Deal for Rockets, Launchers
item.description: Israel will supply Greece with launchers and rockets in a deal valued at €650 million ($750 million), the Israeli Ministry of Defense said in a statement.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Dip Buyers Return Amid Ceasefire Hopes
item.description: US equity futures edged higher and crude oil slipped as investors focused on signs of a potential diplomatic push toward a ceasefire in the Iran war.

item.title: Oracle Taps Schneider Electric Executive as New CFO
item.description: Oracle Corp. named Schneider Electric SE executive Hilary Maxson as its new chief financial officer, effective immediately.

item.title: India’s Refiners Delay Maintenance to Stabilize Fuel Supplies
item.description: Indian Oil Corp. and Bharat Petroleum Corp. have delayed routine maintenance at some of their units to ensure adequate supplies in the world’s third-largest oil consumer, according to a senior government official.

item.title: Canadian Confidence Hits 11-Month Low as Iran War Drags On
item.description: Canadian consumer confidence fell to its lowest level in almost a year as the war in Iran, which has sent energy prices soaring and stoked worries about persistent inflation, entered its sixth week.

item.title: Chile Private Debt Attract New Cash Even as US Investors Seek Exits
item.description: SURA Investments and BTG Pactual Chile launched new private-credit funds in Chile, expanding in an industry that has set off alarm bells in the US after years of extraordinary growth.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Boot Barn, Carvana, Dow, Netflix, Kratos
item.description: US equity-index futures are mixed as traders weigh the latest developments in the war in the Middle East. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are up 0.1% at 7:40 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Qatar LNG Vessels U-Turn After Attempt to Pass Through Hormuz
item.description: Two tankers carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appear to have abandoned an effort to exit the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz — delaying what would have been the first exports to buyers outside of the region since the war started.
